task: Add input validation to the login() function in src/auth.js so it rejects an empty username with a clear error.

input.query: Add input validation to login() function in src/auth.js to reject empty username

input.title: Auth convention: normalize username, then reject empty
input.learning: login in src/auth.js normalizes the username via normalizeUsername (trim + lowercase) and then rejects an empty normalized key by throwing Error("username must not be empty") before any db.check call. Normalization still happens first so mixed-case emails resolve correctly, and whitespace-only inputs are caught by the post-normalization emptiness check rather than passed through to credential lookup.
input.evidence: src/auth.js:3-9 — login normalizes then throws on empty key before db.check

input.reason: login now also rejects empty normalized usernames; new packet captures both the normalization rule and the post-normalization emptiness check.
login now also rejects empty normalized usernames; new packet captures both the normalization rule and the post-normalization emptiness check.
